items.final_wording: Specifies history, physical examination, and clinician-selected initial investigations for chronic persistent breathlessness.

source.title: Adult breathlessness pathway (pre-diagnosis): diagnostic pathway support tool
source.population: Adults with chronic persistent breathlessness, primarily symptoms lasting more than eight weeks.
source.exclusions: Exact presentation-level source for chronic adult breathlessness; international only and not directly validated for UAE practice or acute presentations.

exact_location.heading: Diagnostic pathway — clinical assessment and initial investigations

source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies history, physical examination, and clinician-selected initial investigations for chronic persistent breathlessness.

items.rationale: The exact NHS England chronic adult breathlessness assessment section supports onset, progression, functional impact, associated-history, relevant-negative and clinician-risk documentation for persistent adult breathlessness; it does not cover acute, paediatric, pregnancy, postoperative or traumatic presentations.

items.final_wording: Specifies onset and associated history, functional impact, exposures, medication context, vital signs, respiratory effort, cardiac and chest examination, and selected investigations.

source.title: Adult breathlessness pathway (pre-diagnosis): diagnostic pathway support tool
source.population: Adults with chronic persistent breathlessness, primarily symptoms lasting more than eight weeks.
source.exclusions: Exact presentation-level source for chronic adult breathlessness; international only and not directly validated for UAE practice or acute presentations.

exact_location.heading: Key points — initial presentation, examination, and investigations

source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies onset and associated history, functional impact, exposures, medication context, vital signs, respiratory effort, cardiac and chest examination, and selected investigations.

items.rationale: The exact key-points section supports recording measured vital signs, clinician-performed chest or cardiovascular assessment and already reviewed investigation status without diagnosing a cause or ordering tests.

items.final_wording: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
source.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
source.setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

items.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC emergency history and disposition material supports clinician-recorded current severity, appearance, abnormal observations, plan, discussion and handover in acute care; it does not determine the diagnosis, intervention, urgency or disposition.

items.final_wording: Lists chest pain, haemoptysis, cyanosis, inability to speak in sentences, confusion, unilateral leg swelling, stridor, rapid progression, and low oxygen saturation.

source.title: Adult breathlessness pathway (pre-diagnosis): diagnostic pathway support tool
source.population: Adults with chronic persistent breathlessness, primarily symptoms lasting more than eight weeks.
source.exclusions: Exact presentation-level source for chronic adult breathlessness; international only and not directly validated for UAE practice or acute presentations.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists chest pain, haemoptysis, cyanosis, inability to speak in sentences, confusion, unilateral leg swelling, stridor, rapid progression, and low oxygen saturation.

items.rationale: The red-flag section was reviewed as assessment-prompt evidence only; no red flag, diagnosis or escalation is selected automatically.
